qids.subject: Operating System
qids.prose: A concurrent system consists of processes using a shared resource in a non-preemptible and mutually exclusive manner. The processes have unique priorities in the range , being the highest priority. It is required to synchronize the processes such that the resource is always allocated to the highest

qids.subject: Operating System
qids.prose: When the result of a computation depends on the speed of the processes involved, there is said to be A. cycle stealing B. race condition C. a time lock D. a deadlock

qids.subject: Operating System
qids.prose: A certain processor provides a 'test and set' instruction that is used as follows: TSET register, flag This instruction atomically copies flag to register and sets flag to . Give pseudo-code for implementing the entry and exit code to a critical region using this instruction.
